@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubearmor-1179-index-out-of-bound-on-containers-with-short-names",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubearmor: Index out of bound on containers with short names",
+    "description": "Index out of bound on containers with short names. Users encounter: \"runtime error: slice bounds out of range\".",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify kubearmor troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your kubearmor deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor -n kubearmor --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for error: `runtime error: slice bounds out of range`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubearmor configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubearmor configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor\nkubectl get configmap -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubearmor\n```\n## Bug Report\n\nI've spawned kuberarmor on a kubernetes cluster, but noticed one pod constantly dying with the error:\n```\npanic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range \n```\nAfter some hours of despair while debugging I found out that a container"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Index out of bound on containers with short names",
+        "description": "Hey , You are correct that the logic for verifying that the container ID is at least 12 characters long is flawed, which is why KubeArmor is not functioning properly. We are working on resolving this issue.\n```yaml\npanic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Index out of bound on containers with short names is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor -n kubearmor --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n kubearmor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that `runtime error: slice bounds out of range` no longer appears in logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hey , You are correct that the logic for verifying that the container ID is at least 12 characters long is flawed, which is why KubeArmor is not functioning properly. We are working on resolving this issue.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "panic: runtime error: slice bounds out of range",
+        "res.Container.ID:405b4c292a7a6afcedd56f6874173dd865d49798a347762649689e40a6ef3b15， container name:405b4c292a7a",
+        "karmor install\nctr run --rm -d docker.io/library/debian:latest cont1\nwaht to see see logs for the node running the created container?like kubectl logs `kubectl logs kubearmor-4zw68  -n kube-system -f`"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
